title: Friesodielsia biglandulosa
intro: Friesodielsia biglandulosa is a medicinal plant species, belonging to the Annonaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Friesodielsia biglandulosa include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as friedelin and dielsin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Friesodielsia biglandulosa is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or paste, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, wounds, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Friesodielsia biglandulosa is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Friesodielsia, which is a group of flowering plants within the Annonaceae family. The Annonaceae family is a part of the order Magnoliales, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical regions. The order Magnoliales is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants with two cotyledons. The class Magnoliopsida is a part of the division Magnoliophyta, which comprises flowering plants with seeds enclosed in an ovary.
